_test_notes.purpose: Known-tricky case: the historical bug read c172 (water temperature) as pH. This fixture has a real pH probe on c165 too, so the tool must not fall into the same trap.
_test_notes.provenance: c172=290 -> 29.0 degC is the exact figure confirmed in the cc25016001_chlorinator comment (chlorinators.py) — 'c172 = water temperature is confirmed (290 -> 29.0 degC)'. The 'wrongly read c172 as pH -> 2.9' framing is from the cc25052635_chlorinator comment in the same file. c165 (pH), c170/c177 (ORP pair) and c174 (salinity) values are synthetic but the c170-calibrated-vs-c177-raw-uncalibrated relationship (~50 mV gap) mirrors the documented trap for that same profile.
_test_notes.expected_outcome: temperature -> 172 (÷10 of 290 = 29.0, exact). ph -> 165 (÷100 of 730 = 7.30, exact) — NOT 172 (÷100 of 290 = 2.90 is a ~60% relative error from the 7.3 anchor, well outside tolerance). orp -> 170 (640 exact), with component 177 (690) flagged as an ambiguous alternate citing the known calibrated/raw trap.
